title: Build Your Own — Custom Implant from Scratch
prerequisites: Advanced Offensive Track - Mastery Tier Access
learning_objectives: Implant architecture design covering core, modules, and C2 protocol
Custom C2 protocol development with encryption and anti-replay
Modular architecture for extensibility without recompilation
Core functionality: check-in, tasking loop, command execution
Advanced evasion: sleep obfuscation and detection bypass
Code quality and peer review preparation
scenario: This Mastery mission requires professional-grade execution in Pan-African corporate network with modern security stack. Challenge: Design and build custom implant from architecture through deployment against Windows 10+ with Defender and EDR. Relevance: African enterprises increasingly deploy EDR; custom implants bypass vendor-specific signatures

scenario_context.setting: Pan-African corporate network with modern security stack
scenario_context.challenge: Design and build custom implant from architecture through deployment against Windows 10+ with Defender and EDR
scenario_context.relevance: African enterprises increasingly deploy EDR; custom implants bypass vendor-specific signatures

subtasks.title: Architecture Design
subtasks.description: Complete implant architecture: core, modules, C2 protocol, OPSEC.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Design multi-region infrastructure:
        - Team server placement: Where to host command and control?
        - Redirectors: How to proxy traffic through multiple layers?
        - Engagement isolation: How to separate 3 concurrent operations?
        - Network isolation: How to prevent cross-engagement contamination?

        Deliver: Architecture document with network diagrams.

subtasks.title: Core Development
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Develop core implant functionality:
        - Check-in: Implant identifies itself to C2
        - Tasking loop: Receive, execute, report results
        - 3 basic commands: e.g., execute, file operations, process listing
        - Build instructions: How to compile for Windows

        Deliver: Source code, build documentation, test evidence.
subtasks.evidence_required: Core source + build instructions + test evidence

subtasks.title: Secure C2 Protocol
subtasks.description: Encrypted comms: authentication, anti-replay, configurable jitter.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Design and implement C2 protocol:
        - Encryption: Use AES-256 or equivalent
        - Authentication: Ensure mutual authentication
        - Anti-replay: Prevent replay attacks
        - Configurability: Allow jitter, callback intervals

        Deliver: Protocol specification, implementation code, test suite.

subtasks.title: Module Development
subtasks.description: 3 modules: file ops, process listing/injection, screenshot.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Implement 3 modules:
        1. File operations: Read, write, delete, directory listing
        2. Process management: List processes, inject code
        3. Screenshot: Capture desktop and send to C2

        Deliver: Module source code, module loader, integration tests.

subtasks.title: Evasion Layer
subtasks.description: Sleep obfuscation + one additional evasion technique.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Implement evasion techniques:
        - Sleep obfuscation: Hide thread waits from detection
        - Additional technique: JMP RET sled, stack spoofing, or equivalent
        - Measure effectiveness: Test against Windows Defender

        Deliver: Evasion code, detection testing results, bypass proof.
subtasks.evidence_required: Evasion code + detection testing vs Defender

subtasks.title: Server Component
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Develop operator-side C2 server:
        - Listener: Accept implant connections
        - Task dispatch: Send commands to implants
        - Result collection: Receive output from implants
        - Basic UI: Menu-driven interface or web dashboard

        Deliver: Server source code, usage instructions, interaction demo.

subtasks.title: Operator Manual
subtasks.description: Complete operator manual for the implant.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Write comprehensive operator guide:
        - Deployment procedures with step-by-step instructions
        - Usage patterns and common workflows
        - OPSEC guidelines for operator behavior
        - Configuration and customization
        - Troubleshooting and recovery procedures

        Deliver: 5+ page manual with screenshots and examples.

subtasks.title: Peer Review Preparation
subtasks.description: Package for code review with self-assessment.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Package for code review:
        - Code organization and documentation
        - Self-assessment: What you're proud of, what needs work
        - Improvement roadmap: Future enhancement plans
        - Known limitations and design tradeoffs

        Deliver: Review package with improvement prioritization.

resources.title: Mission Overview: Build Your Own — Custom Implant from Scratch

reflection_prompt: How would your implant architecture change if targeting diverse African networks (limited bandwidth, air-gapped, etc.)? What telemetry would indicate your custom implant is being detected by specific EDR vendors?

quiz.question: In custom implant architecture, what is the primary purpose of modular design with plugins?
options: To make the implant larger in file size
To allow operators to load only necessary functionality without recompiling the entire implant, improving flexibility and OPSEC
To guarantee the implant will never be detected
To eliminate the need for C2 communication
quiz.explanation: Modular architecture allows operators to customize implants for specific targets (load only Android malware components, or only cloud tools) without recompilation. This reduces artifact size, improves flexibility, and enhances OPSEC by avoiding unnecessary code on the target.

quiz.question: When designing a C2 protocol for a custom implant deployed in African enterprise networks, what anti-replay mechanism would best prevent defender replay attacks?
options: Include timestamps or sequence numbers that are validated server-side to prevent old commands from being replayed
Send the same authentication token every time for simplicity
Use the implant's hostname as the only identifier
quiz.explanation: Anti-replay mechanisms prevent defenders from capturing legitimate implant traffic and replaying it. Timestamps, nonces, or sequence numbers ensure that old commands can't be reused, providing protection against network-level attacks and EDR replay detection.

portfolio_artifacts: Evidence of technical excellence and strategic thinking
mentor_notes:
This Mastery mission requires senior mentor review. Candidates must demonstrate:
- Professional-grade execution and documentation
- Deep understanding of African organizational and threat contexts
- Strategic thinking beyond tactical execution
- Readiness for commercial red team operations
- Leadership potential within OCH and broader community

Successful completion positions candidate for Mastery portfolio and professional practice.
